page: Microsoft Azure E128-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture, consisting of 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the E128-32as_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3789.97 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 41.52 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 59904.22 and a PassMark CPU score of 57357.48. Memory performance is balanced, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 102.69 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is the complete absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring reliance on external storage solutions. Qualitatively, the high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes it a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require proportional scaling of compute resources. It is suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and data analytics workloads.

This high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive compute scaling. It is suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
